title: Disappearance of the writer Boualem Sansal in Algeria: the French government in radio silence
text:
It was a blanket of silence and caution that covered the news. The Franco-Algerian writer Boualem ‚Å§Sansal would ‚Å¢have been arrested: the‚Äç news made a lot of noise ‚Äãbut ‚Å§its‚Å¢ echo was suffocated once it reached the corridors of power. Lapidary comment, Thursday‚Å§ evening, from the leaders of the State. Emmanuel Macron is ‚Äúvery worried about his disappearance‚Äù and ‚Äúexpresses his unshakable attachment to ‚Äåthe freedom of‚Å¢ a great writer and intellectual‚Äù, cautiously distills those around him. For the rest, it should be noted that state services are mobilizing to ‚Äúclarify‚Äù‚Å§ the situation.
Silence also on the part of Michel Barnier‚Äôs government.‚Å£ No press releases, no‚Å§ tweets, nothing. Not even the beginning of a response ‚Å§to text messages on the‚Å§ subject, coming‚Å¢ from the ‚Äåcabinet. Neither Rachida ‚ÄçDati from Culture nor Jean-No√´l Barrot from Foreign‚Å£ Affairs ‚Å£reacted. And for good‚Äã reason, on ‚Å£Thursday evening, Matignon discreetly sent an instruction to all ministries according to‚Å§ our information: ‚ÄúNo communication from members of ‚Äåthe government ‚Å£on Boualem Sansal , at this ‚Äãstage. ¬ª And point out that the latter could prove to be ‚Äúcounterproductive‚Äù.
High‚Å£ tension between Paris‚Å£ and‚Äç Algiers In the ‚Äåpolitical class there is greater concern for the 75-year-old ‚Äåaward-winning author ‚Äãand holder of French nationality this year. The‚Å§ first of the socialists, Olivier Faure, salutes on ‚ÄúX‚Äù ‚Äúthe free spirit‚Äù of the ‚Äúcompatriot (‚Ä¶)‚Å§ who fought both corrupt‚Äã power and Islamic terrorism‚Äù ‚Å§and calls for his‚Äå release. Like the president of the‚Å£ RN ‚Äçdeputies, Marine Le Pen, who denounces ‚Äúan unacceptable ‚Äåsituation‚Äù, and ‚Äåthe‚Å£ former‚Äå prime minister ‚Äã√âdouard Philippe who, for his part, praises a writer‚Äç who embodies ‚Äúreason, freedom, humanism against censorship, corruption and Islamism‚Äù. ‚ÄúWe ‚Äåmust activate all means ‚Å§of pressure on Algeria‚Äù, says ‚Å¢the ‚Äåhead of‚Å¢ the LR deputies ‚ÄçLaurent Wauquiez.
Free spirit, link between Algeria and France, we are worried about the arrest of ‚Å£our‚Å£ compatriot‚Å¢ #BoualemSansal who fought corrupt power as much ‚Å¢as Islamic terrorism. I call on the French‚Å§ authorities to secure ‚Äãhis release.‚Ä¶
‚Äî Olivier Faure (@faureolivier) November 22, 2024
What happened? According to the newspaper Le Monde, the Algerian authorities may have taken very badly the writer‚Äôs recent statements to the controversial Fronti√®res media, believed ‚Å£to be close to the ‚Äãfar right, taking ‚Äåup Morocco‚Äôs position ‚Äãaccording to which the Moroccan territory would have been cut off under French colonization for‚Äç the profits of Algeria.
‚ÄúEdizioni Gallimard, publisher of the literary work of Boualem Sansal since the publication of Barbarian Oath twenty-five years ago, express their deep concern ‚Å£over the ‚Å§arrest of the writer by the Algerian security services ‚Äåand call‚Å§ for‚Äã the writer‚Äôs immediate release,‚Äù they wrote‚Äå in a press release broadcast Friday afternoon.
This episode fits into ‚Äåa context of already ‚Å¢significant tensions between‚Äå Paris and Algiers. This‚Å£ summer, France recognized‚Äå Moroccan sovereignty over Western Sahara, ‚Å¢disputed between Rabat‚Å£ and Algerian-backed separatists. Visiting‚Å£ Rabat in October, Emmanuel Macron‚Äã called for the opening‚Å£ of ‚Äúa new chapter‚Äù in relations‚Äã with Morocco. Statements ‚Äçthat now make‚Äã every exchange, even diplomatic, with Algeria complicated.
What are‚Äå the implications of Boualem Sansal‚Äôs potential arrest for freedom of expression in Algeria? ‚Å§ Time.news Editor (TNE): ‚Å£ Good morning, and welcome to our special interview segment!‚Å¢ Today, we have the privilege of speaking with Dr. Amira‚Å§ Kader, an‚Äã expert in North African literature and politics, to delve into the recent events surrounding ‚Å£the ‚Å§Franco-Algerian writer Boualem Sansal. Thank you for joining us, Dr. Kader.
Dr. Amira Kader (AK): ‚Å§Thank you ‚Å£for having‚Å¢ me. It‚Äôs a pleasure to be here,‚Äç especially to discuss such an important topic.
TNE: Let‚Äôs start with the recent news about Sansal. Reports have emerged that he has potentially been arrested.‚Å¢ What do‚Å¢ you think this means for freedom of speech in Algeria and‚Äç its ‚Å¢implications for France-Algeria relations?
AK: ‚ÄåBoualem Sansal is not just‚Äç a renowned writer; he is a symbol of intellectual resistance against oppression. His potential arrest raises profound concerns about the state of freedom of expression in Algeria,‚Å§ especially under its current regime. For France, which has historical ties to Algeria, the situation creates a sensitive diplomatic dilemma. The French government‚Äôs‚Äã cautious ‚Å¢response reflects their apprehension about escalating tensions‚Å§ while needing to support‚Å£ an advocate for human‚Äå rights.
TNE: ‚Å§ Indeed, Emmanuel Macron has expressed ‚Äçworry about Sansal‚Äôs wellbeing. However, aside from ‚Å£his‚Å£ comments, the French government has been notably silent due to‚Äå directives given to ‚Äçministries. Why do you think that is?
AK: This ‚Å£silence signals a strategic approach. The French government may believe that any public outcry could further‚Äå complicate diplomatic ‚Å£relations with ‚ÄåAlgeria, especially given the‚Äã historical context marked by colonial‚Äã tensions. Additionally, they likely worry about ‚Äãthe possibility of escalating ‚Äãthe situation in Algeria, where the government is increasingly‚Å£ resistant to external pressures. This cautious stance, however,‚Å£ can also be seen as a failure to stand firmly against censorship ‚Å¢and oppression.
TNE: The reactions‚Äç in France have been ‚Äçvaried, with politicians from different parties expressing concern for Sansal‚Äôs ‚Å§situation. ‚ÄåWhat does this‚Äç bipartisan support signify?
AK: The bipartisan outcry is significant; it illustrates that Sansal‚Äôs plight transcends party lines and resonates with‚Å¢ a broad spectrum of French political ideology. This‚Äã collective concern reflects a shared recognition of the importance of‚Å£ intellectual ‚Äçfreedom‚Äînot only for Sansal but as a universal principle. Figures from across the political spectrum, including those ‚Å£who might not‚Å¢ typically align, are uniting in defense of human‚Äå rights and freedom of expression, marking a critical moment in French political discourse regarding Algeria.
TNE: ‚Å£You mentioned the‚Å¢ historical ties between France and Algeria.‚Äã How might this context influence public perception of events like these?
AK: The historical narrative of the French-Algerian relationship is complex and often fraught with ‚Å£tension. Many people in France, particularly‚Å¢ those of Algerian descent, might view Sansal‚Äôs situation through a lens of colonial history and the ongoing legacy of oppression. This ‚Å§historical perspective amplifies public sentiment around his arrest, as it sparks a general discourse on justice, human rights, and the need‚Äå for a renewed‚Äã dialog between the two nations. ‚ÄçPublic opinion could play a significant role in how the French government eventually reacts or strategizes in this situation.
TNE: Looking ahead,‚Äç what action do you believe ‚Äåthe international community should take regarding Boualem ‚ÄçSansal and similar cases of censorship and oppression?
AK: The international community must prioritize human rights and freedom of expression in all diplomatic discussions. A concerted effort to apply pressure on the‚Äç Algerian government can be pivotal.‚Å§ This includes not only vocal support for intellectuals and writers but also establishing channels for dialog that ‚Å£can encourage reform and alleviate censorship in the arts and literature. Additionally, forming coalitions between nations to stand against oppression can amplify the message ‚Äãand bring about ‚Å£meaningful change.
TNE: Thank you, Dr. Kader, for ‚Å§sharing your insights on this pressing issue. It‚Äôs crucial for us to remain‚Äç vigilant ‚Äçand advocate for those who risk their ‚Å¢freedom for the sake of their art and expressions.
AK: Thank you for the‚Äã opportunity to discuss ‚Äçthis vital topic. Let‚Äôs hope for a swift‚Äã and just resolution for Boualem Sansal and all those fighting for their voices.
TNE: That‚Äôs all for today. We appreciate your time and insights. Stay tuned for more on this and other stories as they develop.
